title: [Comb] AddOp canonicalizer hangs in an infinite loop
body:
Consider the following input:
```mlir
hw.module private @foo() {
  %false = hw.constant false
  %c0_i32 = hw.constant 0 : i32
  %c1_i32 = hw.constant 1 : i32
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // 3 preds: ^bb0, ^bb2, ^bb3
    llhd.wait (%false : i1), ^bb2
  ^bb2:  // pred: ^bb1
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb3(%c0_i32 : i32), ^bb1
  ^bb3(%0: i32):  // 2 preds: ^bb2, ^bb8
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb4(%c0_i32 : i32), ^bb1
  ^bb4(%1: i32):  // 2 preds: ^bb3, ^bb7
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb5, ^bb8
  ^bb5:  // pred: ^bb4
    cf.cond_br %false, ^bb6, ^bb7
  ^bb6:  // pred: ^bb5
    cf.br ^bb7
  ^bb7:  // 2 preds: ^bb5, ^bb6
    %2 = comb.add %1, %c1_i32 : i32
    cf.br ^bb4(%2 : i32)
  ^bb8:  // pred: ^bb4
    cf.br ^bb3(%c0_i32 : i32)
  }
  hw.output
}
```
Running this through `circt-opt --canonicalize --debug` gets stuck in an infinite loop, with a ton of `comb.add`-related messages:
```
[some control flow rewriting]

//===-------------------------------------------===//
Processing operation : 'comb.add'(0x6000005bc010) {
  %35 = "comb.add"(%35, %34) : (i32, i32) -> i32


  * Pattern  : 'comb.add -> ()' {
    ** Insert  : 'comb.add'(0x6000000b3a90)
    ** Replace : 'comb.add'(0x6000005bc010)
    ** Modified: 'comb.add'(0x6000000b3a90)
    ** Modified: 'comb.add'(0x6000005bc010)
    ** Erase   : 'comb.add'(0x6000005bc010)
  } -> success : pattern applied successfully
} -> success : at least one pattern matched
//===-------------------------------------------===//

[above repeats 33 times]

//===-------------------------------------------===//
Processing operation : 'comb.add'(0x6000000b3a90) {
  %35 = "comb.add"(%35) : (i32) -> i32

} -> success : operation was folded
//===-------------------------------------------===//
** Modified: 'comb.add'(0x6000000b3a90)
// *** IR Dump After Successful Folding ***
llhd.process {
  cf.br ^bb1
^bb1:  // 3 preds: ^bb0, ^bb2, ^bb3
  llhd.wait (%false : i1), ^bb2
^bb2:  // pred: ^bb1
  cf.br ^bb1
^bb3(%0: i32):  // pred: ^bb6
  cf.br ^bb1
^bb4:  // no predecessors
  cf.br ^bb6
^bb5:  // no predecessors
  cf.br ^bb6
^bb6:  // 2 preds: ^bb4, ^bb5
  %c2_i32 = hw.constant 2 : i32
  %c4_i32 = hw.constant 4 : i32
  %c8_i32 = hw.constant 8 : i32
  %c16_i32 = hw.constant 16 : i32
  %c32_i32 = hw.constant 32 : i32
  %c64_i32 = hw.constant 64 : i32
  %c128_i32 = hw.constant 128 : i32
  %c256_i32 = hw.constant 256 : i32
  %c512_i32 = hw.constant 512 : i32
  %c1024_i32 = hw.constant 1024 : i32
  %c2048_i32 = hw.constant 2048 : i32
  %c4096_i32 = hw.constant 4096 : i32
  %c8192_i32 = hw.constant 8192 : i32
  %c16384_i32 = hw.constant 16384 : i32
  %c32768_i32 = hw.constant 32768 : i32
  %c65536_i32 = hw.constant 65536 : i32
  %c131072_i32 = hw.constant 131072 : i32
  %c262144_i32 = hw.constant 262144 : i32
  %c524288_i32 = hw.constant 524288 : i32
  %c1048576_i32 = hw.constant 1048576 : i32
  %c2097152_i32 = hw.constant 2097152 : i32
  %c4194304_i32 = hw.constant 4194304 : i32
  %c8388608_i32 = hw.constant 8388608 : i32
  %c16777216_i32 = hw.constant 16777216 : i32
  %c33554432_i32 = hw.constant 33554432 : i32
  %c67108864_i32 = hw.constant 67108864 : i32
  %c134217728_i32 = hw.constant 134217728 : i32
  %c268435456_i32 = hw.constant 268435456 : i32
  %c536870912_i32 = hw.constant 536870912 : i32
  %c1073741824_i32 = hw.constant 1073741824 : i32
  %c-2147483648_i32 = hw.constant -2147483648 : i32
  %c0_i32_0 = hw.constant 0 : i32
  %1 = comb.add %1 : i32
  cf.br ^bb3(%c0_i32 : i32)
}

[above repeats indefinitely]
```
It looks like the canonicalizer initially rewrites the control flow to:
```
hw.module private @foo() {
  %false = hw.constant false
  %c0_i32 = hw.constant 0 : i32
  %c1_i32 = hw.constant 1 : i32
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // 3 preds: ^bb0, ^bb2, ^bb3
    llhd.wait (%false : i1), ^bb2
  ^bb2:  // pred: ^bb1
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb3(%0: i32):  // pred: ^bb6
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb6:  // 2 preds: ^bb4, ^bb5
    %1 = comb.add %1, %c1_i32 : i32
    cf.br ^bb3(%c0_i32 : i32)
  }
  hw.output
}
```
That recursive add shouldn't really be expanded 🤔

@TaoBi22 This looks like the recursive mux canonicalizers you've been fixing in the past.

hw.module private @foo() {
  %false = hw.constant false
  %c0_i32 = hw.constant 0 : i32
